channel.title: iDigital
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from iDigital, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: The Download: Chicago’s surveillance network, and building better bras
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Inside Chica...

item.title: Inside Chicago’s surveillance panopticon
item.description: Early on the morning of September 2, 2024, a Chicago Transit Authority Blue Line train was the scene of a random and horrific mass shooting. Four peop...

item.title: Fictiv and MISUMI report on the state of manufacturing, supply chain digitalization
item.description: MISUMI Americas finds that AI, digital platforms, and regional resilience are priorities for industrial modernization. The post Fictiv and MISUMI repo...

item.title: Integrated motion control enables sophisticated robot motion
item.description: Integrated motion control allows all kinds of robots to tackle a range of tasks with precision, in contrast with conventional machinery. The post Inte...

item.title: Ghost Robotics: Innovating for safety
item.description: Gavin Kenneally, co-founder and CEO of Ghost Robotics, discusses the design and function of the Vision 60 quadruped robots in the latest podcast episo...

item.title: Visual drone detection moves into critical infrastructure playbooks
item.description: Learn how operators of critical infrastructure are leveraging advanced technologies to combat the increasing commonality of drone-related incidents. T...

item.title: NORD releases digital twin simulation platform for robotics developers
item.description: NORD said it allows engineers to verify whether the drive concept is suitable for the system early in the planning phase. The post NORD releases digit...

item.title: The Download: Microsoft’s online reality check, and the worrying rise in measles cases
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Microsoft ha...

item.title: Exclusive eBook: The great Al hype correction of 2025
item.description: 2025 was a year of reckoning, including how the heads of the top AI companies made promises they couldn’t keep. In this exclusive subscriber-only eBoo...

item.title: Job titles of the future: Breast biomechanic
item.description: Twenty years ago, Joanna Wakefield-Scurr was having persistent pain in her breasts. Her doctor couldn’t diagnose the cause but said a good, supportive...

item.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
item.description: Railway , a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thurs...

item.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
item.description: The artificial intelligence coding revolution comes with a catch: it's expensive. Claude Code , Anthropic's terminal-based AI agent that can write, de...

item.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
item.description: Alfred Wahlforss was running out of options. His startup, Listen Labs , needed to hire over 100 engineers, but competing against Mark Zuckerberg's $10...

item.title: Salesforce rolls out new Slackbot AI agent as it battles Microsoft and Google in workplace AI
item.description: Salesforce on Tuesday launched an entirely rebuilt version of Slackbot , the company's workplace assistant, transforming it from a simple notification...

item.title: Anthropic launches Cowork, a Claude Desktop agent that works in your files — no coding required
item.description: Anthropic released Cowork on Monday, a new AI agent capability that extends the power of its wildly successful Claude Code tool to non-technical users...

item.title: Best Practices for Building the AI Development Platform in Government

item.title: Advance Trustworthy AI and ML, and Identify Best Practices for Scaling AI
item.description: By John P. Desmond, AI Trends Editor Advancing trustworthy AI and machine learning to mitigate agency risk is a priority for the US Department of Ener...

item.title: Promise and Perils of Using AI for Hiring: Guard Against Data Bias
item.description: By AI Trends Staff While AI in hiring is now widely used for writing job descriptions, screening candidates, and automating interviews, it poses a ris...

item.title: Predictive Maintenance Proving Out as Successful AI Use Case
item.description: By John P. Desmond, AI Trends Editor More companies are successfully exploiting predictive maintenance systems that combine AI and IoT sensors to coll...

item.title: Novelty In The Game Of Go Provides Bright Insights For AI And Autonomous Vehicles
item.description: By Lance Eliot, the AI Trends Insider We already expect that humans to exhibit flashes of brilliance. It might not happen all the time, but the act it...
